message:
Update beads: close login command task (already implemented)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4dx","title":"Implement theme provider and dark mode toggle","description":"**Details:** - ThemeProvider wrapping app for dark/light mode - Persists preference to localStorage - Respects system preference on first visit - Dark mode toggle in Nav component - CSS variables switch based on theme **Implementation:** ```typescript // apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx 'use client'; import { createContext, useContext, useEffect, useState } from 'react'; type Theme = 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'; export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { const [theme, setTheme] = useState('system'); useEffect(() => { const stored = localStorage.getItem('theme') as Theme; if (stored) setTheme(stored); }, []); useEffect(() => { const root = document.documentElement; const isDark = theme === 'dark' || (theme === 'system' && window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches); root.classList.toggle('dark', isDark); localStorage.setItem('theme', theme); }, [theme]); return ( {children} ); } ``` **Toggle Component:** - Sun/Moon icon button - Cycles through light → dark → system - Tooltip showing current mode **Location:** - apps/web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx - apps/web/src/components/theme-toggle.tsx **Reasoning:** Dark mode is expected in developer tools. System preference respect shows attention to detail. Persistent preference provides consistent UX.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:34:00.527000897-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:08:43.738433267-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ThemeProvider with light/dark/system modes, ThemeToggle component, integrated into Providers.tsx. Ready for Nav component to include the toggle. Build and tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4dx","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vsk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:03.111792364-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4fv","title":"Implement bundles.ts with Bundle type definitions","description":"Part 13.1 of PLAN. Define Bundle interface: id, title, description, version, updatedAt, promptIds array, workflow description, whenToUse array, author, featured flag, icon (Lucide name). Create bundles array with 'getting-started' and 'documentation-suite' bundles. Derive featuredBundles, bundlesById Map. Helpers: getBundle(id), getBundlePrompts(bundleId), generateBundleSkillMd(bundle). Bundles are curated collections enabling one-click install of related prompts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:43:57.179699458-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T01:18:25.622214942-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T01:18:25.622214942-05:00","close_reason":"Implementation complete and verified with type checking","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4fv","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-oqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:48:16.040149419-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4fv","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-x21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:48:16.068173663-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4q8k","title":"EPIC: CLI Authentication & Premium Integration","description":"## Overview Add optional authentication to the `jfp` CLI tool, allowing users to seamlessly access their premium account data including custom prompts, collections, saved/bookmarked prompts, and notes. ## Why This Matters The CLI is the primary interface for power users and AI agents. By adding authentication: - Premium users get their full library in the CLI - Users can access their collections and saved prompts - The "swap meet" community prompts become available - Notes and annotations sync across devices - Search spans all content (public + personal) ## Design Principles ### 1. Transparent for Non-Users - All existing functionality works WITHOUT login - No nag screens, no forced auth, no degraded experience - Login is purely additive - it unlocks MORE, never restricts - Clear, honest messaging about what premium provides ### 2. Works Everywhere - Local machines: Browser-based OAuth with auto-callback - Remote/SSH: Device code flow (6-character code) - Headless servers: Same device code flow - CI/CD: Token-based auth via environment variable ### 3. Secure & Private - Tokens stored securely in user's config directory - Short-lived access tokens with refresh capability - No passwords stored locally - Easy revocation via web dashboard ## Authentication Flows ### Flow 1: Local Machine (Default) ``` User runs: jfp login ↓ CLI starts local HTTP server on random port (e.g., 19847) ↓ CLI opens browser to: https://premium.jeffreysprompts.com/cli/auth?port=19847 ↓ User completes Google OAuth on premium site ↓ Premium site redirects to: http://localhost:19847/callback?token=xxx ↓ CLI receives token, stores it, shows success message ↓ Local server shuts down ``` ### Flow 2: Remote Machine (--remote or auto-detected) ``` User runs: jfp login --remote (or CLI detects no display) ↓ CLI requests device code from: POST /api/cli/device-code ↓ API returns: { device_code: "abc123", user_code: "XYZW-1234", verification_url: "..." } ↓ CLI shows: "Visit https://premium.jeffreysprompts.com/cli/verify and enter code: XYZW-1234" ↓ User visits URL, logs in with Google, enters code ↓ Meanwhile, CLI polls: POST /api/cli/device-token with device_code ↓ Once user completes, API returns access token ↓ CLI stores token, shows success ``` ### Flow 3: CI/CD / Programmatic ``` User sets: JFP_TOKEN=xxx in environment ↓ CLI detects env var, uses it for all authenticated requests ↓ No interactive login needed ``` ## New CLI Commands ### `jfp login` - Initiates authentication flow - Options: `--remote` (force device code flow) - Stores credentials in `~/.config/jfp/credentials.json` ### `jfp logout` - Clears stored credentials - Optionally revokes token on server ### `jfp whoami` - Shows current authentication status - Displays: email, tier, token expiry ### `jfp list --mine` - Lists only user's custom prompts ### `jfp list --saved` - Lists user's bookmarked/saved prompts ### `jfp collections` - Lists user's collections - `jfp collections ` shows prompts in collection ### `jfp notes ` - Shows notes for a prompt - `jfp notes --add "note text"` adds a note ## API Endpoints Needed (Premium Site) ### Auth Endpoints - `POST /api/cli/device-code` - Initiate device code flow - `POST /api/cli/device-token` - Exchange device code for token - `POST /api/cli/token/refresh` - Refresh access token - `POST /api/cli/token/revoke` - Revoke token (logout) ### Data Endpoints (Authenticated) - `GET /api/cli/me` - Current user info - `GET /api/cli/prompts` - All prompts (public + user's) - `GET /api/cli/prompts/mine` - User's custom prompts only - `GET /api/cli/prompts/saved` - User's saved/bookmarked prompts - `GET /api/cli/collections` - User's collections - `GET /api/cli/collections/:id` - Collection details with prompts - `GET /api/cli/notes/:promptId` - Notes for a prompt - `POST /api/cli/notes/:promptId` - Add note to prompt ## Token Format Using JWT with claims: - `sub`: User ID - `email`: User email - `tier`: Subscription tier (free/premium) - `exp`: Expiration time - `iat`: Issued at time ## Credential Storage Location: `~/.config/jfp/credentials.json` ```json { "access_token": "eyJ...", "refresh_token": "xxx", "expires_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z", "email": "user@example.com", "tier": "premium" } ``` ## Success Criteria - `jfp login` works on local machines with browser - `jfp login --remote` works on headless/SSH machines - Authenticated users see their prompts in `jfp list` - `jfp search` finds results in personal prompts - Token refresh happens automatically - Logout clears all credentials - Non-authenticated users have identical experience to before","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:08:45.460885074-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:08:45.460885074-05:00"} -{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4szj","title":"Public site: add subtle Pro teaser block + FAQ entry","description":"## Goal Add a subtle, tasteful Pro teaser that explains the value in 1–2 lines and links to the Pro site. Add a brief FAQ entry clarifying free + open-source vs. optional Pro. ## Placement - **Landing page:** lower third or near footer, below core browsing/search content. - **Component location:** likely `apps/web/src/app/page.tsx` (or the marketing section it uses). ## UX Requirements - **Non-intrusive:** should not distract from browsing prompts. - **Compact:** a small card, muted banner, or inline callout. - **Free-first language:** emphasize public site stays free and open-source. ## Copy Draft (editable) **Heading:** “Pro mode (optional)” or “Upgrade to Pro” **Body:** “Keep your favorites, add notes, and access early prompts. Everything on the public site stays free and open-source.” **Button:** “See Pro features” → `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com` ## FAQ Addition (if FAQ exists) - **Q:** Is the public site free and open-source? - **A:** Yes. The core library and browsing experience are open-source. Pro is optional and adds personal features like bookmarks, notes, and early-access prompts. ## Implementation Notes - Use existing UI primitives (Card/Callout/Banner) to stay consistent. - Avoid new dependencies or complex layout. - If there’s a `MarketingSection` or similar component, place the teaser there. - Keep typography smaller than hero content. ## Accessibility - Ensure heading uses appropriate semantic level (e.g., `h3`). - Button should be keyboard focusable and have adequate contrast. ## Acceptance Criteria - A subtle Pro teaser appears on the landing page. - A short FAQ entry explains free vs. Pro. - All links point to `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`. - No layout regression on mobile. ","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:12:44.53187785-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:21:39.898885509-05:00"} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4szj","title":"Public site: add subtle Pro teaser block + FAQ entry","description":"## Goal Add a subtle, tasteful Pro teaser that explains the value in 1–2 lines and links to the Pro site. Pro is optional and adds personal features like bookmarks, notes, and early-access prompts. ## Implementation Notes - Use existing UI primitives (Card/Callout/Banner) to stay consistent. - Avoid new dependencies or complex layout. - If there’s a `MarketingSection` or similar component, place the teaser there. - Keep typography smaller than hero content. ## Accessibility - Ensure heading uses appropriate semantic level (e.g., `h3`). - Button should be keyboard focusable and have adequate contrast. ## Acceptance Criteria - A subtle Pro teaser appears on the landing page. - A short FAQ entry explains free vs. Pro. - All links point to `https://pro.jeffreysprompts.com`. - No layout regression on mobile. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:12:44.53187785-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:22:32.379728357-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T20:22:32.379728357-05:00","close_reason":"Added subtle Pro teaser block and FAQ section to landing page - responsive design with accessible markup, links to pro.jeffreysprompts.com"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4wbk","title":"Task: SpotlightSearch component unit tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:44:40.982845392-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:14:30.054711667-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:14:30.054711667-05:00","close_reason":"Added SpotlightSearch unit tests (open/search/navigation/copy)","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4wbk","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wxw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:55:02.021105277-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4x3","title":"Build StatsDashboard component for transcript page","description":"Part 10.5 of PLAN. 6-stat grid: Duration (clock icon), Messages (user+Claude count), Tool Calls count, Files Edited count, Lines Written count, Tokens Used (formatted as M). Each stat card has icon, label, value, optional detail text. Uses framer-motion staggered animation. Responsive grid (2→3→6 columns).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:45:13.582288766-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T02:07:04.698221584-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T02:07:04.698221584-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented StatsDashboard with 6-stat grid, icons, and staggered animations","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4x3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cke","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:48:47.738396098-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-4x3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-vsk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:48:47.765590358-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-50cn","title":"Feature: E2E Logging Infrastructure","description":"Shared logging infrastructure for all E2E tests. TestLogger class with debug/info/step/error levels, timestamps, and context formatting. CLI spawn wrapper that captures and logs stdout/stderr with timing. Playwright step logger plugin for detailed web test logging. Log file output for CI artifact collection. Screenshot-on-step option for visual debugging.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:43:38.40841383-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:28:20.846154398-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:28:20.846154398-05:00","close_reason":"E2E Logging Infrastructure complete: TestLogger, CliRunner, PlaywrightLogger all implemented with levels, timestamps, file output, and CI artifact support","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-50cn","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-0rg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:53:42.02629474-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} @@ -114,6 +114,7 @@ {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cs7","title":"Build RelatedPrompts component for permalink pages","description":"**Details:** - Shows related prompts based on tag overlap + BM25 similarity - Displayed at bottom of /prompts/[id] pages - Click navigates to related prompt - Limited to 4-6 suggestions **Implementation:** ```typescript // apps/web/src/components/related-prompts.tsx interface RelatedPromptsProps { promptId: string; limit?: number; } export function RelatedPrompts({ promptId, limit = 5 }: RelatedPromptsProps) { const related = useMemo(() => { const current = getPrompt(promptId); if (!current) return []; // Score by tag overlap + BM25 on title/description const candidates = prompts.filter(p => p.id !== promptId); const scored = candidates.map(p => ({ prompt: p, score: calculateTagOverlap(current.tags, p.tags) + bm25Score(current.title + ' ' + current.description, p) })); return scored .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score) .slice(0, limit) .map(s => s.prompt); }, [promptId, limit]); if (related.length === 0) return null; return (

Related Prompts

{related.map(p => ( ))}
); } ``` **Scoring Algorithm:** - Tag overlap: +1 point per shared tag - BM25: Score title+description against current prompt - Weighted sum: 60% tags, 40% BM25 **Location:** apps/web/src/components/related-prompts.tsx **Reasoning:** Related prompts increase engagement and help users discover more content. Tag overlap ensures topical relevance while BM25 adds semantic similarity.","notes":"Added RelatedPrompts component and wired into prompt permalink content","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:35:23.87036225-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:41:34.215887393-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:41:34.2158915-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-cs7","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-z16","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:54.781837612-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-czs","title":"Implement jfp bundles and bundle show -- Details: bundles lists all bundles with prompt counts. bundle show displays description, included prompts, workflow, when-to-use. --bundle flag on install creates combined skill.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:27:22.127698832-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:58:30.639704239-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:58:30.639704239-05:00","close_reason":"CLI bundles + install --bundle already implemented","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-czs","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-wxb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:37:44.410701829-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-d4z","title":"Implement hash-embedder.ts for fallback embeddings","description":"Part 14.2 of PLAN. Implement hashEmbed(input, dims=128) that produces deterministic fixed-length vectors using fast non-crypto hash (xxhash/murmur). No model download required. Good enough for lightweight fallback similarity when semantic model unavailable. Pure TypeScript, no dependencies.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T00:44:18.198181366-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T16:59:18.718495709-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T16:59:18.718495709-05:00","close_reason":"hash-embedder.ts already implemented"} +{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-del9","title":"E2E Tests: CLI Authentication Flow","description":"Create comprehensive E2E tests for CLI authentication covering both local browser and remote device code flows. Test Scenarios: - Local browser flow: happy path, port conflicts, timeout, browser launch failure - Remote device code flow: happy path, code expiration, slow poll, denied, invalid code - Token persistence: save/load credentials, auto-refresh, re-login on expiry - Integration tests: full login->whoami->list->logout flow Logging Requirements: - Log each test step with timestamps - Log HTTP requests/responses (sanitize tokens) - Log credential file operations - Log browser/process spawning events - Dump full state on failure for debugging Test Infrastructure: - Use Bun test runner - Mock HTTP server for premium site endpoints - Temporary credential directory per test - Cleanup hooks for all resources ","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:22:12.82362631-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:22:51.836286881-05:00"} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-dfj3","title":"Task: Add E2E test matrix for multiple browsers","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:49:33.124260073-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:49:33.124260073-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-dfj3","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:56:46.488500706-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-dgmm","title":"Task: Add Web E2E tests (Playwright) to GitHub Actions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:49:21.232317309-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T19:17:11.01564325-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T19:17:11.01564325-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented in ci.yml and release.yml","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-dgmm","depends_on_id":"jeffreysprompts.com-9sj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-10T17:56:36.19247828-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-dio8","title":"Implement automatic token refresh in CLI","description":"## Task Add automatic token refresh to the CLI when access tokens expire. ## Why Users shouldn't have to manually re-login every 24 hours. The CLI should: 1. Detect when access token is expired (or about to expire) 2. Automatically use refresh token to get new access token 3. Only prompt for re-login when refresh token also expires (30 days) ## Implementation ### Update `packages/cli/src/lib/credentials.ts` ```typescript const PREMIUM_URL = process.env.JFP_PREMIUM_URL ?? "https://premium.jeffreysprompts.com"; /** * Get a valid access token, refreshing if necessary * Returns null if not logged in or refresh fails */ export async function getValidAccessToken(): Promise { // Check environment variable first (CI/CD) const envToken = process.env.JFP_TOKEN; if (envToken) { return envToken; } const creds = await loadCredentials(); if (!creds) { return null; } // If not expired, return current token if (!isExpired(creds)) { return creds.access_token; } // Try to refresh if (creds.refresh_token) { const refreshed = await refreshAccessToken(creds.refresh_token); if (refreshed) { // Save new credentials await saveCredentials(refreshed); return refreshed.access_token; } } // Refresh failed or no refresh token return null; } /** * Attempt to refresh the access token */ async function refreshAccessToken(refreshToken: string): Promise { try { const response = await fetch(`${PREMIUM_URL}/api/cli/token/refresh`, { method: "POST", headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }, body: JSON.stringify({ refresh_token: refreshToken, client_id: "jfp-cli", }), }); if (!response.ok) { // Refresh failed - token likely expired or revoked return null; } const data = await response.json(); return { access_token: data.access_token, refresh_token: data.refresh_token, expires_at: data.expires_at, email: data.email, tier: data.tier, user_id: data.user_id, }; } catch { // Network error - return null to fail gracefully return null; } } /** * Check if credentials need refresh (expired or expiring soon) */ export function needsRefresh(creds: Credentials): boolean { if (!creds.expires_at) return false; const expiresAt = new Date(creds.expires_at); const now = new Date(); // Refresh if expiring within 5 minutes const bufferMs = 5 * 60 * 1000; return now.getTime() > expiresAt.getTime() - bufferMs; } ``` ### Update API Client The API client should use `getValidAccessToken()` instead of directly reading credentials: ```typescript // packages/cli/src/lib/api-client.ts import { getValidAccessToken, isLoggedIn } from "./credentials"; export async function authenticatedFetch( url: string, options: RequestInit = {} ): Promise { const token = await getValidAccessToken(); const headers = new Headers(options.headers); if (token) { headers.set("Authorization", `Bearer ${token}`); } return fetch(url, { ...options, headers, }); } ``` ## User Experience - If token refreshes successfully: User never notices, command works - If refresh fails: User sees "Session expired. Please run 'jfp login' to sign in again." - Never show technical error details to user ## Logging Add debug logging for troubleshooting: ```typescript if (process.env.JFP_DEBUG) { console.error("[jfp] Access token expired, attempting refresh..."); console.error("[jfp] Refresh successful, new token expires:", creds.expires_at); } ``` ## Verification - Token auto-refreshes before expiry - Commands continue working after refresh - Clear error message when refresh fails - JFP_DEBUG shows refresh activity","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-10T20:19:43.451531077-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T20:19:43.451531077-05:00"}

@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@ "use client"; import { Suspense, useMemo, useCallback, useState, useEffect } from "react"; -import { AlertTriangle } from "lucide-react"; +import { AlertTriangle, Sparkles, ChevronDown } from "lucide-react"; import { prompts, categories, tags } from "@jeffreysprompts/core/prompts/registry"; import { searchPrompts } from "@jeffreysprompts/core/search/engine"; import { Hero } from "@/components/Hero"; @@ -199,6 +199,61 @@ function HomeContent() { + {/* Pro Teaser & FAQ Section */} +
